system: two coupled RLC resonators with one observed flux node and damping recorded outside storage

practical: node voltage on resonator 1 integrated or phase-referenced to infer flux_phi1
linewidth or Q-factor as damping metadata

control_variables.name: R1
control_variables.meaning: visible resonator resistance recorded as damping

control_variables.name: R2
control_variables.meaning: hidden resonator resistance recorded as damping

regime_assumptions: linear inductive coupling is a valid quadratic storage model
R1 and R2 are dissipative response parameters and are not inserted into H
drive and active feedback are outside this card
the visible block H_vv is declared as the ceiling for hidden load
flux coordinates are the declared storage coordinates
equation_hook: U = flux_phi1^2/(2 L1) + flux_phi2^2/(2 L2) + (flux_phi1 - flux_phi2)^2/(2 Lc); R1,R2 are damping metadata outside U
coordinate_note: Both active coordinates are flux coordinates in the same units; damping parameters are deliberately outside the Hessian and hidden-load call.

module_object.description: 2x2 coupled inductive storage Hessian with one visible and one hidden flux coordinate

derivation_status: exact for the declared coupled LC storage model; exact damping response is not claimed

ceiling_or_reference.description: T = H_vv, the visible flux stiffness before hidden relaxation

positive_scope: This card preserves the coupled-LC hidden-renormalisation story while making damping a recorded practical response feature rather than a static curvature term.
boundary: It does not describe dissipative linewidths, driven frequency response, nonlinear coupling, or measurement back-action.
failure_mode: The attachment fails if damping is folded into H or if the resonators cannot be approximated by the declared passive quadratic storage coordinates.
next_measurement: Measure node response while varying coupling; interpret storage geometry separately from linewidth or Q-factor.
